raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I stopped at XXXX # XXXX in XXXX XXXX, NV, to fill my car with gas. The charge was {$34.00}. When I got my monthly statement from CITIBANK XXXX it showed a charge of {$76.00}, an over charge of {$42.00}. I submitted a dispute to CITIBANK and provided a copy of my original receipt. Their Case # is XXXX. CITIBANK investigated and reported back that the merchant says it was a valid charge ; my dispute was denied, case closed, I have tried to call the merchant but all I get is a message saying the mail box is full ; I couldn't ever leave a message. I wrote a letter to the merchant requestng a credit or refund ; I have not received an answer. 
I have talked to CITIBANK " Customer Service '' several times and received letters from them saying the merchant claims the charge is valid. They say there is nothing they can do. They say that if I don't pay the full amount they will report me to the three credit reporting agencies. Reluctantly I paid the full amount. 
I feel that CITIBANK owes me {$42.00} because that's who I had to send my payment to. 
I always thought using a credit card provided some safety from fraud. That is not the case with CITIBANK.
